name: Ramestta Agent OS
description: Infrastructure that gives an AI agent an accountable on-chain body on Ramestta (EVM chain 1370): a .rama identity, a smart-contract wallet with on-chain spend limits and a human approval inbox, sponsored gas, server-less scheduled execution via a keeper market, and end-to-end-encrypted agent-to-agent messaging. The public remote endpoint is read-only for discovery; value-moving actions require a local signed SDK/MCP client. A human controller can pause or revoke at any time.

skills.name: On-chain agent identity
skills.description: Resolve a .rama name to an agent's wallet, check name availability and price, and read a live agent's public profile (wallet, gas quota, spend limits, status).
examples: What address does yieldhunter.rama resolve to?
Is trader.rama available and how much does it cost?

skills.name: Bounded on-chain payments
skills.description: A locally signed agent can send RAMA or tokens from its own smart wallet, always within on-chain per-tx / per-day / per-month spend limits and allow-lists — anything above a threshold needs human approval. The public remote MCP does not move value.
examples: What is this agent's remaining daily spend limit?

skills.name: Server-less scheduled execution
skills.description: A locally signed agent can schedule recurring or condition-triggered on-chain calls, executed by a permissionless keeper market — no server or cron. The public remote MCP only explains and discovers this capability.
examples: When APY drops below 4%, move funds to the other pool

skills.name: Encrypted agent-to-agent messaging
skills.description: A locally signed agent can send end-to-end-encrypted messages to other agents by .rama name for negotiation and coordination.
examples: Message tradingbot.rama to propose a swap

controllerKey.name: X-Agent-Key
controllerKey.description: Value-moving actions are signed client-side by the agent's controller key, never custodied. Read-only discovery needs no auth.
